name: Zerodha-Style Order Validator — Parameterized Test Suite
scenario: Write a comprehensive parameterized test suite for a stock order validation system. Orders must be validated against multiple rules: symbol format, quantity limits, price ranges, order type, and fund sufficiency. Using all four @Source annotations, achieve thorough boundary and edge case coverage with minimal test code duplication.
description: The production `OrderValidator` is provided. Students write a parameterized test suite using @CsvSource for tabular cases, @MethodSource for complex Order objects, @EnumSource for order types, @NullAndEmptySource for null handling — covering all validation rules, all boundaries, and all enum values.

core.description: @CsvSource test: `isValid_csvCases` with at least 8 rows covering all validation rules. Columns: symbol, qty, price, orderType, availableFunds, expectedValid. Use descriptive test name template.
@MethodSource test: `validate_complexOrders` accepting `Order` objects. Provide at least 4 cases including boundary orders. Method returns Stream<Arguments>.
@EnumSource test: `validate_allOrderTypes_nonNullReturn` verifying that getOrderFeeRate(OrderType) returns a non-negative value for ALL order types.
@NullAndEmptySource test: `validate_nullOrEmptySymbol_returnsFalse` verifying null and empty symbols are rejected.
Boundary value test: @CsvSource for quantity boundaries (0, 1, 2, 999, 1000, 1001) and price boundaries (0, 0.01, 1000000, 1000000.01).
At least one @ParameterizedTest uses custom name template: `@ParameterizedTest(name = 'Case {index}: {0} qty={1} → {5}')` or equivalent.
All test executions pass. Total: at least 20 test executions across all parameterized tests.

bonus.description: Add @CsvFileSource loading test cases from `src/test/resources/order-test-cases.csv`. File has at least 5 rows of test data.
Add a failure message test: use `assertThrows` inside a @ParameterizedTest and verify the exact exception message contains the right field name (qty, price, symbol) in the failure message.
Add a custom `ArgumentsProvider` implementing `ArgumentsProvider` interface for generating 10 random valid orders and 10 random invalid orders. Verify each category is handled correctly.

data_to_use:
Order validation rules:
- symbol: non-null, non-blank, 2-10 uppercase letters only
- qty: 1-1000
- price: > 0 and < 10,000,000
- orderType: BUY or SELL
- availableFunds (for BUY): must be >= qty * price

enum OrderType { BUY, SELL }
Fee rates: BUY=0.1%, SELL=0.2%

Boundary values:
  qty: 0 (invalid), 1 (valid), 1000 (valid), 1001 (invalid)
  price: 0 (invalid), 0.01 (valid), 9999999.99 (valid), 10000000 (invalid)
example_output:
Test Results (OrderValidatorTest):
  isValid_csvCases: 8 executions ✓
  validate_complexOrders: 4 executions ✓
  validate_allOrderTypes_feeNonNegative: 2 executions ✓  (BUY, SELL)
  validate_nullOrEmptySymbol_returnsFalse: 2 executions ✓
  validate_qtyBoundaries: 6 executions ✓
  validate_priceBoundaries: 4 executions ✓

Total: 26 test executions, 0 failures
ai_policy_explanation: Limited to 5 hints. This is a testing exercise — read the production code carefully. @CsvSource column order must match test method parameter order. @MethodSource must be static. @EnumSource(OrderType.class) without names= iterates all values. @NullAndEmptySource gives null then empty string.
submission_instructions: Save as OrderValidatorTest.java. Push to GitHub. Include the production code in src/main/java, test in src/test/java.
tips_for_success: @MethodSource method must be static and return Stream<Arguments>
Boundary pattern: test {n-1, n, n+1} at each limit
@EnumSource(OrderType.class) — no names= parameter means ALL values
concepts_reinforced: Boundary value analysis at all limits
